RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ARNOLD GREENBERG AND DOREEN GREENBERG'S Motion For Order Appointing Discovery Referee For The Deposition Of James Rigney , Compelling Production Of Documents And For Sanctions - Mr. Rigney must appear to continue his deposition on or before 9/1/11. The deposition will continue day-to-day until completed. Breaks will be by mutual agreement and not while a question is pending. His counsel may state an objection, but may not explain or argue the objection or makes a speaking objection or instruct not to answer except on grounds of privilege. Mr. Rigney will answer questions to the extent of his knowledge or understanding including what he has learned from others and including questions concerning his business entity. The original documents will be made available during the deposition. A referee will not be appointed at this time, without prejudice to renewing the request if there are further problems. The parties are directed to read and follow the Court's Guidelines for Professional Conduct concerning depositions, available on the Court's Web site. The court did not award sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported. = (301/PJB)
